user_message: Can you work with me on forecast prep for Evergreen Solutions? This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 08:28 EST
Subject: Re: forecast prep Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 16:47 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Evergreen Solutions forecast prep

Just checking in on the forecast prep proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-09 17:53 EST
Subject: URGENT: forecast prep Issue at Evergreen Solutions

There's been a change in our timeline for forecast prep. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Evergreen Solutions: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-N trap (Decision-log format doesn't carry thread-attribution); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-N failure mode (Decision-log format doesn't carry thread-attribution)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
